findings.title: pnpm audit reports high severity advisories in Next.js and Kysely
findings.repro: Run `pnpm audit --json`; see `docs/regression-audit/logs/audit.json`. It exits non-zero with 8 high, 6 moderate and 3 low advisories, including Next.js <16.2.6 and Kysely <0.28.17.
findings.impact: Public launch would ship known high severity framework advisories, including App Router middleware/proxy bypass, SSRF/DoS classes, and Kysely JSON-path injection risk through drizzle-orm.
findings.suggestedFix: Upgrade Next.js to a patched version satisfying the audit advisories, and update drizzle-orm/Kysely once compatible patched versions are available; rerun `pnpm audit` and the full verify chain.

findings.title: GameRoom race-condition test is flaky under playtest
findings.repro: `pnpm playtest` failed once with expected deadPlayers `[race-3]` but received `[]`; immediate rerun passed. Logs: `docs/regression-audit/logs/playtest.log` and `docs/regression-audit/logs/playtest-rerun.log`.
findings.impact: CI/release verification can fail nondeterministically, and the test may be advancing the narrator before the concurrent night submissions are fully processed.
findings.suggestedFix: Make the test wait for both server-side submissions to be accepted before `narratorAdvance`, or add an explicit deterministic helper/ack path for the race-condition contract.

findings.title: playtest suite filter does not narrow Vitest to the intended three suites
findings.repro: `pnpm playtest` output ran all 7 GameRoom tests, including `GameRoom.race-conditions.test.ts`, although `scripts/playtest.mjs` lists only three suites. The spawned command includes a literal `--` after the package script.
findings.impact: `pnpm playtest` no longer means the documented multi-client regression subset and can inherit unrelated launch-coverage flakiness.
findings.suggestedFix: Adjust pnpm/Vitest argument forwarding so the selected suite paths are passed as Vitest filters without the extra literal separator.

findings.title: Auth E2E reports success while skipping database-backed flows
findings.repro: Run `pnpm e2e:auth` without `DATABASE_URL`. It logs `пропуснато: DATABASE_URL липсва` for email registration, authenticated redirect return, and account deletion, then exits 0. CI (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) does not provide a Postgres service.
findings.impact: The standard `pnpm verify` gate can pass without exercising the highest-risk public-launch auth flows.
findings.suggestedFix: Provide a CI Postgres service and seedable test database for `e2e:auth`, or make skipped critical auth flows fail outside an explicitly marked local-only mode.

findings.title: Default 404 page leaks English UI text
findings.repro: Open `/this-does-not-exist` or inspect `docs/regression-audit/runtime-probe-results.json`; body text includes `This page could not be found.`
findings.impact: Violates the Bulgarian-only UI invariant on a public route.
findings.suggestedFix: Add a custom `apps/web/app/not-found.tsx` with Bulgarian copy and links back to the main game surfaces.

findings.title: Game-server safe errors use Latin `host` in user-facing messages
findings.repro: Send privileged commands as a non-host client; safe errors include `Само host-ът...` at lines 350, 406, and 444.
findings.impact: These safe errors are sent to clients and can appear in the UI, violating the Bulgarian-only copy invariant.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace `host-ът` with Bulgarian wording such as `домакинът` in all user-facing error strings.

findings.title: Role assignment can fall back to Math.random
findings.repro: Inspect `defaultRandomSource`; when `globalThis.crypto.getRandomValues` is unavailable, it returns `Math.random()`.
findings.impact: Violates the project invariant that role assignment must be crypto-based. In a crypto-less runtime/polyfill failure, roles become predictable.
findings.suggestedFix: Use Node crypto as a server fallback or throw a Bulgarian-safe configuration/runtime error instead of falling back to `Math.random`.

findings.title: Werewolves and Vampires win conditions used wipe-out instead of documented parity rule
findings.repro: Run 2 werewolves vs 2 villagers through `evaluateWinCondition`; pre-fix it returned `{ winner: null }`, while `docs/rules-bg.md:667` documents parity wins. UI role-card copy in `apps/web/components/play-room-client.tsx:2213` already promised "Върколаците да достигнат паритет със селото".
findings.impact: Games continued 2+ phases past the documented endgame, frustrating play and contradicting UI promises.
findings.suggestedFix: Fixed in branch `fix/win-condition-parity` (commit `cd020a1`). Werewolves and vampires now win at parity; mixed WW+Vampires scenario resolves via faction headcount tie-break.

findings.title: Achievements API exposes arbitrary user achievements by query parameter
findings.repro: Call `GET /api/achievements?userId=<any-user-id>`; the route reads the user id from the URL and does not call `auth.api.getSession`. The route test at `apps/web/app/api/achievements/__tests__/route.test.ts:48` codifies this behavior.
findings.impact: Personal achievements can be enumerated for any known user id despite `/achievements` being auth-gated.
findings.suggestedFix: Require a Better Auth session in the route and use `session.user.id`; reject mismatched `userId` parameters or remove the parameter entirely.

findings.title: Game-token route signs normalized codes without enforcing room-code length
findings.repro: POST `{ "code": "ABC" }` to `/api/game-token` with a valid session; line 14 normalizes and line 16 only rejects empty strings.
findings.impact: The API can mint tokens for impossible or malformed room codes, pushing validation failure later to the game-server connect path.
findings.suggestedFix: Validate `roomCode` with the same 4-12 uppercase alphanumeric rule used by the join UI before signing.

findings.title: Account deletion conflicts with game-history foreign keys
findings.repro: `/api/account/delete` calls Better Auth `deleteUser` at `apps/web/app/api/account/delete/route.ts:23`, but `games.hostId`, `game_players.userId`, and `game_events.actorId/targetId` reference `user.id` without cascade or set-null behavior.
findings.impact: Users with persisted games may receive `Не успяхме да изтрием профила.` and remain undeleted, undermining the GDPR deletion flow.
findings.suggestedFix: Define an account deletion policy: anonymize game-history user references before deletion, or change selected FKs to `onDelete: set null`/cascade where legally and product-wise correct.

findings.title: Runtime secret validation is weaker than the documented 32-character invariant
findings.repro: `assertUsableSecret` rejects only secrets shorter than 16 characters, while `AGENTS.md` and `scripts/check-production-env.mjs` require 32+ characters. `apps/web/lib/env.ts:7` also validates `BETTER_AUTH_SECRET` with min 16.
findings.impact: Local/test runtimes can accidentally exercise weaker-than-production secrets, and the invariant is enforced inconsistently across layers.
findings.suggestedFix: Align runtime validation and env schema with the 32-character minimum, while keeping explicit test fixtures long enough.

findings.title: Docker production environment omits Google OAuth and NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL
findings.repro: Inspect the `web.environment` block: it passes Discord OAuth and `NEXT_PUBLIC_GAME_SERVER_URL`, but not `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET`, or `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL`. `scripts/check-production-env.mjs:51` warns only about Discord OAuth.
findings.impact: A Docker deployment can silently ship without Google sign-in despite the public-launch UI presenting Google as a primary auth path; URL-dependent metadata/client config can also fall back unexpectedly.
findings.suggestedFix: Pass Google OAuth variables and `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` into the web service, and extend production env checks to require or explicitly warn on Google OAuth.

findings.title: Production CSP still allows unsafe inline/eval scripts and non-TLS connect targets
findings.repro: Inspect the `Content-Security-Policy` header: `script-src` includes `'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval'`, and `connect-src` includes `http:` and `ws:`.
findings.impact: The reverse proxy has useful baseline headers, but CSP is permissive enough to weaken XSS and mixed-content defense for a public launch.
findings.suggestedFix: Tighten CSP after confirming Next/Sentry requirements: remove `unsafe-eval` in production, move toward nonce/hash-based scripts, and restrict `connect-src` to HTTPS/WSS origins.

findings.title: Legacy AuthForm component remains unused after sign-in redesign
findings.repro: Run `rg -n 'AuthForm|auth-form' apps/web`; the only hits are the component definition and its own CSS class.
findings.impact: Dead auth UI code can confuse future changes and still contains pre-redesign behavior/copy surface.
findings.suggestedFix: Delete `auth-form.tsx` and any orphaned `.auth-form` CSS after confirming no tests or docs depend on it.

findings.title: Launch-visible stats TODOs remain in landing quickstart
findings.repro: Run `rg -n 'TODO|FIXME|HACK|XXX|@deprecated' apps packages scripts docs`; TODOs remain at lines 182 and 214 for family/byFamily stats, plus `packages/shared/src/game-config.ts:66` for promo rules.
findings.impact: Not a blocker, but the home quickstart data story still has known incomplete stats wiring.
findings.suggestedFix: Either wire the missing stats fields or move the TODOs to a tracked issue and simplify the code comments.

findings.title: AGENTS.md command and stack notes drift from current scripts
findings.repro: Inspect `AGENTS.md`: auth stack mentions Discord but not Google; `pnpm test` still says 39 tests; `pnpm verify` omits `frontend:e2e`, `e2e:auth`, `visual`, and `perf:budget`; hook docs reference `.husky` while the repo uses `.githooks/pre-commit`.
findings.impact: New agents and contributors can rely on stale launch instructions and miss the real verification chain.
findings.suggestedFix: Update AGENTS.md to match current auth providers, test counts, verify chain, and hook location.
